about the request (e.g., \"GET /api/users\")\n137\t//\n138\t// Returns a ValidationError with appropriate suggestions.\n139\t//\n140\t// Example usage:\n141\t//\n142\t//\terr := validate.FormatStatusCodeError(200, 404, \"GET /api/users/123\")\n143\t//\t// Output:\n144\t//\t// status_code validation failed\n145\t//\t//   Expected: 200 (OK)\n146\t//\t//   Actual:   404 (Not Found)\n147\t//\t//   Context:  GET /api/users/123\n148\t//\t//   Suggestions:\n149\t//\t//     - Verify the endpoint URL is correct\n150\t//\t//     - Check if the resource ID or identifier exists\n151\t//\t//     - Ensure the resource hasn't been deleted or moved\n152\tfunc FormatStatusCodeError(expected interface{}, actual int, context string) ValidationError {\n153\t\treturn NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code\").\n154\t\t\tWithExpected(expected).\n155\t\t\tWithActual(actual).\n156\t\t\tWithContext(context).\n157\t\t\tFormat()\n158\t}\n159\t\n160\t// FormatErrorMessageError creates a validation error for error message pattern mismatches.\n161\t//\n162\t// Parameters:\n163\t//   - expectedPattern: The pattern that was expected (e.g., \"invalid.*token\", \"not found\")\n164\t//   - actualMessage: The actual error message received\n165\t//   - fieldName: The field where the error was found (e.g., \"error\", \"message\")\n166\t//   - context: Optional context about the validation\n167\t//\n168\t// Returns a ValidationError with appropriate suggestions.\n169\t//\n170\t// Example usage:\n171\t//\n172\t//\terr := validate.FormatErrorMessageError(\"invalid.*token\", \"access_denied\", \"error\", \"OAuth validation\")\n173\t//\t// Output:\n174\t//\t// error_message validation failed\n175\t//\t//   Expected: invalid.*token\n176\t//\t//   Actual:   access_denied\n177\t//\t//   Context:  OAuth validation\n178\t//\t//   Field:    error\n179\t//\t//   Suggestions:\n180\t//\t//     - Review the error message for specific details\n181\t//\t//     - Check API documentation for this error type\n182\t//\t//     - Verify request parameters match requirements\n183\tfunc FormatErrorMessageError(expectedPattern, actualMessage, fieldName, context string) ValidationError {\n184\t\treturn NewValidationFormatter(\"error_message\").\n185\t\t\tWithExpected(expectedPattern).\n186\t\t\tWithActual(actualMessage).\n187\t\t\tWithFieldName(fieldName).\n188\t\t\tWithContext(context).\n189\t\t\tFormat()\n190\t}\n191\t\n192\t// FormatStatusCodeRangeError creates a validation error for status code range mismatches.\n193\t//\n194\t// Parameters:\n195\t//   - pattern: The range pattern (e.g., \"4xx\", \"5xx\", \"2xx\")\n196\t//   - actual: The actual status code received\n197\t//   - context: Optional context about the validation\n198\t//\n199\t// Returns a ValidationError with appropriate range information and suggestions.\n200\t//\n201\t// Example usage:\n202\t//\n203\t//\terr := validate.FormatStatusCodeRangeError(\"4xx\", 200, \"error response check\")\n204\t//\t// Output:\n205\t//\t// status_code_range validation failed\n206\t//\t//   Expected: 4xx (400-499)\n207\t//\t//   Actual:   200\n208\t//\t//   Context:  error response check\n209\t//\t//   Suggestions:\n210\t//\t//     - Review request parameters for errors\n211\t//\t//     - Check authentication credentials\n212\t//\t//     - Verify the resource exists and is accessible\n213\tfunc FormatStatusCodeRangeError(pattern string, actual int, context string) ValidationError {\n214\t\tmin, max, desc, err := getRangeInfo(pattern)\n215\t\n216\t\t// Build range info string\n217\t\trangeInfo := fmt.Sprintf(\"%d-%d\", min, max)\n218\t\tif desc != \"\" {\n219\t\t\trangeInfo = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d-%d (%s)\", min, max, desc)\n220\t\t}\n221\t\n222\t\tdetails := []string{}\n223\t\tif err != nil {\n224\t\t\tdetails = append(details, fmt.Sprintf(\"Pattern error: %v\", err))\n225\t\t} else {\n226\t\t\tdetails = append(details, fmt.Sprintf(\"Status code %d is outside range %d-%d\", actual, min, max))\n227\t\t}\n228\t\n229\t\tformatter := NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code_range\").\n230\t\t\tWithExpected(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s (%s)\", pattern, desc)).\n231\t\t\tWithActual(actual).\n232\t\t\tWithContext(context).\n233\t\t\tWithValidationDetails(details...)\n234\t\n235\t\t// Only add range info if the pattern is valid\n236\t\tif err == nil {\n237\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithRangeInfo(rangeInfo)\n238\t\t}\n239\t\n240\t\treturn formatter.Format()\n241\t}\n242\t\n243\t// FormatContentTypeError creates a validation error for Content-Type header mismatches.\n244\t//\n245\t// Parameters:\n246\t//   - expected: The expected content type (e.g., \"application/json\")\n247\t//   - actual: The actual content type received\n248\t//   - context: Optional context about the validation\n249\t//\n250\t// Returns a ValidationError with appropriate suggestions.\n251\t//\n252\t// Example usage:\n253\t//\n254\t//\terr := validate.FormatContentTypeError(\"application/json\", \"text/html\", \"API response\")\n255\t//\t// Output:\n256\t//\t// content_type validation failed\n257\t//\t//   Expected: application/json\n258\t//\t//   Actual:   text/html\n259\t//   Context:  API response\n260\t//\t//   Suggestions:\n261\t//\t//     - Verify Content-Type header matches request body format\n262\t//\t//     - Check if charset or boundary parameters are needed\n263\t//\t//     - Ensure the body is properly formatted for the content type\n264\tfunc FormatContentTypeError(expected, actual, context string) ValidationError {\n265\t\treturn NewValidationFormatter(\"content_type\").\n266\t\t\tWithExpected(expected).\n267\t\t\tWithActual(actual).\n268\t\t\tWithContext(context).\n269\t\t\tFormat()\n270\t}\n271\t\n272\t// FormatCustomValidationError creates a validation error with full customization.\n273\t//\n274\t// This function allows complete control over all validation error fields, including\n275\t// custom suggestions. Use this when the predefined convenience functions don't meet\n276\t// your specific requirements.\n277\t//\n278\t// Parameters:\n279\t//   - validationType: The category of validation\n280\t//   - expected: The expected value\n281\t//   - actual: The actual value received\n282\t//   - options: Optional configuration functions to customize the error\n283\t//\n284\t// Example usage:\n285\t//\n286\t//\terr := validate.FormatCustomValidationError(\n287\t//\t    \"custom_field\",\n288\t//\t    \"required_value\",\n289\t//\t    \"actual_value\",\n290\t//\t    validate.WithContext(\"custom validation\"),\n291\t//\t    validate.WithResponseSnippet(`{\"field\": \"actual_value\"}`),\n292\t//\t    validate.WithSuggestions(\"Check field value\", \"Verify configuration\"),\n293\t//\t)\n294\tfunc FormatCustomValidationError(\n295\t\tvalidationType string,\n296\t\texpected, actual interface{},\n297\t\toptions ...FormatOption,\n298\t) ValidationError {\n299\t\tconfig := &FormatConfig{}\n300\t\tfor _, opt := range options {\n301\t\t\topt(config)\n302\t\t}\n303\t\n304\t\tformatter := NewValidationFormatter(validationType).\n305\t\t\tWithExpected(expected).\n306\t\t\tWithActual(actual)\n307\t\n308\t\tif config.Context != \"\" {\n309\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithContext(config.Context)\n310\t\t}\n311\t\tif config.ResponseSnippet != \"\" {\n312\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithResponseSnippet(config.ResponseSnippet)\n313\t\t}\n314\t\tif config.FieldName != \"\" {\n315\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithFieldName(config.FieldName)\n316\t\t}\n317\t\tif config.PatternDetails != \"\" {\n318\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithPatternDetails(config.PatternDetails)\n319\t\t}\n320\t\tif config.RangeInfo != \"\" {\n321\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithRangeInfo(config.RangeInfo)\n322\t\t}\n323\t\tif len(config.ValidationDetails) > 0 {\n324\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithValidationDetails(config.ValidationDetails...)\n325\t\t}\n326\t\tif len(config.Suggestions) > 0 {\n327\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithSuggestions(config.Suggestions...)\n328\t\t}\n329\t\n330\t\treturn formatter.Format()\n331\t}\n332\t\n333\t// =============================================================================\n334\t// FORMAT OPTIONS FOR CUSTOM VALIDATION\n335\t// =============================================================================\n336\t\n337\t// FormatConfig holds configuration options for validation error formatting.\n338\ttype FormatConfig struct {\n339\t\tContext            string\n340\t\tResponseSnippet    string\n341\t\tFieldName          string\n342\t\tPatternDetails     string\n343\t\tRangeInfo          string\n344\t\tValidationDetails  []string\n345\t\tSuggestions        []string\n346\t}\n347\t\n348\t// FormatOption is a function that configures a FormatConfig.\n349\ttype FormatOption func(*FormatConfig)\n350\t\n351\t// WithContext creates a FormatOption that sets the validation context.\n352\tfunc WithContext(context string) FormatOption {\n353\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n354\t\t\tc.Context = context\n355\t\t}\n356\t}\n357\t\n358\t// WithResponseSnippet creates a FormatOption that sets the response snippet.\n359\tfunc WithResponseSnippet(snippet string) FormatOption {\n360\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n361\t\t\tc.ResponseSnippet = snippet\n362\t\t}\n363\t}\n364\t\n365\t// WithFieldName creates a FormatOption that sets the field name.\n366\tfunc WithFieldName(fieldName string) FormatOption {\n367\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n368\t\t\tc.FieldName = fieldName\n369\t\t}\n370\t}\n371\t\n372\t// WithPatternDetails creates a FormatOption that sets pattern details.\n373\tfunc WithPatternDetails(details string) FormatOption {\n374\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n375\t\t\tc.PatternDetails = details\n376\t\t}\n377\t}\n378\t\n379\t// WithRangeInfo creates a FormatOption that sets range information.\n380\tfunc WithRangeInfo(info string) FormatOption {\n381\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n382\t\t\tc.RangeInfo = info\n383\t\t}\n384\t}\n385\t\n386\t// WithValidationDetails creates a FormatOption that adds validation details.\n387\tfunc WithValidationDetails(details ...string) FormatOption {\n388\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n389\t\t\tc.ValidationDetails = append(c.ValidationDetails, details...)\n390\t\t}\n391\t}\n392\t\n393\t// WithSuggestions creates a FormatOption that sets custom suggestions.\n394\tfunc WithSuggestions(suggestions ...string) FormatOption {\n395\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n396\t\t\tc.Suggestions = append(c.Suggestions, suggestions...)\n397\t\t}\n398\t}\n399\t\n400\t// =============================================================================\n401\t// HELPER FUNCTIONS\n402\t// =============================================================================\n403\t\n404\t// getRangeInfo extracts range information from a pattern string.\n405\tfunc getRangeInfo(pattern string) (min, max int, desc string, err error) {\n406\t\tmin, max, err = ParseStatusCodeRange(pattern)\n407\t\tif err != nil {\n408\t\t\treturn 0, 0, \"\", err\n409\t\t}\n410\t\n411\t\tdesc, err = GetStatusCodeRangeDescription(pattern)\n412\t\tif err != nil {\n413\t\t\t// Return parsed range even if description fails\n414\t\t\tdesc = \"\"\n415\t\t}\n416\t\n417\t\treturn min, max, desc, nil\n418\t}\n419\t\n420\t// =============================================================================\n421\t// ERROR TYPE VALIDATION TRACKING\n422\t// =============================================================================\n423\t\n424\t// errorTypeTracker tracks invalid error types encountered during validation.\n425\t// This helps identify typos, deprecated error types, or configuration issues.\n426\ttype errorTypeTracker struct {\n427\t\tmu       sync.RWMutex\n428\t\tinvalidTypes map[string]int // error type -> count\n429\t}\n430\t\n431\t// global tracker instance\n432\tvar globalErrorTypeTracker = &errorTypeTracker{\n433\t\tinvalidTypes: make(map[string]int),\n434\t}\n435\t\n436\t// TrackInvalidErrorType records an invalid error type for later analysis.\n437\t// This is called automatically by FormatError when an error type doesn't\n438\t// match any known ErrorType enum value.\n439\t//\n440\t// Parameters:\n441\t//   - errorType: The invalid error type string that was encountered\n442\t//\n443\t// Returns the current count for this error type (how many times it's been seen).\n444\tfunc TrackInvalidErrorType(errorType string) int {\n445\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Lock()\n446\t\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Unlock()\n447\t\n448\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes[errorType]++\n449\t\treturn globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes[errorType]\n450\t}\n451\t\n452\t// GetInvalidErrorTypes returns a snapshot of all invalid error types encountered\n453\t// and their occurrence counts. This is useful for debugging and identifying\n454\t// configuration issues.\n455\t//\n456\t// Returns a copy of the invalid types map to avoid concurrent modification issues.\n457\tfunc GetInvalidErrorTypes() map[string]int {\n458\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RLock()\n459\t\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RUnlock()\n460\t\n461\t\tresult := make(map[string]int, len(globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes))\n462\t\tfor k, v := range globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes {\n463\t\t\tresult[k] = v\n464\t\t}\n465\t\treturn result\n466\t}\n467\t\n468\t// ResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking clears all tracked invalid error types.\n469\t// This is primarily useful for testing to ensure a clean state between tests.\n470\tfunc ResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking() {\n471\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Lock()\n472\t\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Unlock()\n473\t\n474\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes = make(map[string]int)\n475\t}\n476\t\n477\t// InvalidErrorTypeCount returns the total number of invalid error types tracked\n478\t// (not the total occurrences, but the number of unique invalid types).\n479\tfunc InvalidErrorTypeCount() int {\n480\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RLock()\n481\t\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RUnlock()\n482\t\n483\t\treturn len(globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes)\n484\t}\n485\t\n486\t// =============================================================================\n487\t// BASIC ERROR MESSAGE FORMATTING\n488\t// =============================================================================\n489\t\n490\t// FormatError creates a basic formatted error message string.\n491\t// This is a simplified formatting function for basic error message display.\n492\t// Unlike the ValidationFormatter which returns ValidationError structs,\n493\t// this function returns a simple formatted string for quick error display.\n494\t//\n495\t// This function supports both string-based error types (for backward compatibility)\n496\t// and validates them against the ErrorType enum for type-safe error classification.\n497\t// The ErrorType enum provides consistent error classification across all validation contexts.\n498\t//\n499\t// Error Type Validation:\n500\t//   - String error types are validated against the ErrorType enum (e.g., \"required\", \"format\", \"range\")\n501\t//   - If the error type is not a recognized ErrorType, it is tracked for debugging purposes\n502\t//   - Invalid error types do NOT cause errors - the original string is still used for backward compatibility\n503\t//   - To check what invalid error types have been encountered, use GetInvalidErrorTypes()\n504\t//   - To reset tracking between tests, use ResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking()\n505\t//\n506\t// Parameters:\n507\t//   - errorType: The type/category of error (e.g., \"required\", \"format\", \"status_code\")\n508\t//   - message: The error message content\n509\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred (can be empty string)\n510\t//\n511\t// Returns a formatted error message string with consistent structure.\n512\t// Handles empty/nil inputs gracefully by returning a default format.\n513\t//\n514\t// Example usage:\n515\t//\n516\t//\t// Valid error types (recognized by ErrorType enum)\n517\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\"required\", \"This field is required\", \"email\")\n518\t//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: This field is required\"\n519\t//\n520\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\"format\", \"Invalid email format\", \"\")\n521\t//\t// Returns: \"[format] Invalid email format\"\n522\t//\n523\t//\t// Custom/unknown error types (still work, but are tracked)\n524\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\"custom_validation\", \"Custom check failed\", \"field\")\n525\t//\t// Returns: \"[custom_validation] field: Custom check failed\"\n526\t//\t// \"custom_validation\" is now tracked as an invalid error type\n527\t//\n528\t//\t// Check what invalid types have been encountered\n529\t//\tinvalidTypes := validate.GetInvalidErrorTypes()\n530\t//\t// Returns: map[string]int{\"custom_validation\": 1, ...}\n531\t//\n532\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\"\", \"Something went wrong\", \"\")\n533\t//\t// Returns: \"[error] Something went wrong\"\n534\t//\n535\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\"validation\", \"\", \"email\")\n536\t//\t// Returns: \"[validation] email: email validation failed\"\n537\tfunc FormatError(errorType string, message string, fieldName ...string) string {\n538\t\t// Validate string errorType against ErrorType enum\n539\t\t// This ensures that string-based error types are checked for validity\n540\t\t// while maintaining backward compatibility with any string value\n541\t\tvalidatedType := ErrorTypeFromString(errorType)\n542\t\n543\t\t// Track invalid error types for debugging\n544\t\t// If ErrorTypeFromString returns ErrTypeUnknown but the original wasn't \"unknown\",\n545\t\t// it means we encountered an unrecognized error type string\n546\t\tif validatedType == ErrTypeUnknown && errorType != \"\" && errorType != \"unknown\" {\n547\t\t\tTrackInvalidErrorType(errorType)\n548\t\t}\n549\t\n550\t\t// Extract field name from variadic args\n551\t\tfieldNameStr := \"\"\n552\t\tif len(fieldName) > 0 {\n553\t\t\tfieldNameStr = fieldName[0]\n554\t\t}\n555\t\n556\t\t// Handle empty message - use fallback\n557\t\tif message == \"\" {\n558\t\t\t// Check if field name was provided\n559\t\t\tif fieldNameStr != \"\" {\n560\t\t\t\tmessage = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed\", fieldNameStr)\n561\t\t\t} else {\n562\t\t\t\tmessage = \"(no message provided)\"\n563\t\t\t}\n564\t\t}\n565\t\n566\t\t// Handle empty errorType - use fallback\n567\t\tif errorType == \"\" {\n568\t\t\terrorType = \"error\"\n569\t\t}\n570\t\n571\t\t// Use FormatErrorMessage with the original error type string\n572\t\t// This maintains backward compatibility - we always use the original string\n573\t\t// even if it wasn't a recognized ErrorType enum value\n574\t\treturn FormatErrorMessage(errorType, message, fieldNameStr)\n575\t}\n576\t\n577\t// FormatErrorWithType creates a basic formatted error message string using ErrorType enum.\n578\t// This function provides type-safe error classification using the ErrorType enum\n579\t// instead of string-based error types.\n580\t//\n581\t// This is the preferred method for error formatting when you have an ErrorType enum\n582\t// value, as it ensures consistent error classification and prevents typos.\n583\t//\n584\t// Parameters:\n585\t//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value (e.g., ErrTypeRequired, ErrTypeFormat)\n586\t//   - message: The error message content\n587\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred (can be empty string)\n588\t//\n589\t// Returns a formatted error message string with consistent structure.\n590\t// Handles empty/nil inputs gracefully by returning a default format.\n591\t//\n592\t// Example usage:\n593\t//\n594\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorWithType(validate.ErrTypeRequired, \"Field is required\", \"email\")\n595\t//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: Field is required\"\n596\t//\n597\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorWithType(validate.ErrTypeFormat, \"Invalid email format\", \"\")\n598\t//\t// Returns: \"[format] Invalid email format\"\n599\t//\n600\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorWithType(validate.ErrTypeRange, \"Value out of range\", \"age\")\n601\t//\t// Returns: \"[range] age: Value out of range\"\n602\tfunc FormatErrorWithType(errorType ErrorType, message string, fieldName string) string {\n603\t\t// Convert ErrorType enum to string for formatting\n604\t\terrorTypeStr := errorType.String()\n605\t\n606\t\t// Handle empty message - use fallback\n607\t\tif message == \"\" {\n608\t\t\t// Check if field name was provided\n609\t\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n610\t\t\t\tmessage = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed\", fieldName)\n611\t\t\t} else {\n612\t\t\t\tmessage = \"(no message provided)\"\n613\t\t\t}\n614\t\t}\n615\t\n616\t\t// Use FormatErrorMessage for consistent formatting\n617\t\treturn FormatErrorMessage(errorTypeStr, message, fieldName)\n618\t}\n619\t\n620\t// =============================================================================\n621\t// FIELD REFERENCE QUOTE STYLES\n622\t// =============================================================================\n623\t\n624\t// QuoteStyle defines the quoting style for field names in field references.\n625\ttype QuoteStyle string\n626\t\n627\tconst (\n628\t\t// NoQuote applies no quotes to field names\n629\t\tNoQuote QuoteStyle = \"\"\n630\t\t// SingleQuote applies single quotes around field names\n631\t\tSingleQuote QuoteStyle = \"'\"\n632\t\t// DoubleQuote applies double quotes around field names\n633\t\tDoubleQuote QuoteStyle = \"\\\"\"\n634\t\t// Backtick applies backtick quotes around field names\n635\t\tBacktick QuoteStyle = \"`\"\n636\t)\n637\t\n638\t// FieldRefConfig holds configuration options for field reference formatting.\n639\ttype FieldRefConfig struct {\n640\t\tquoteStyle QuoteStyle\n641\t\tprefix     string\n642\t}\n643\t\n644\t// FieldRefOption is a function that configures a FieldRefConfig.\n645\ttype FieldRefOption func(*FieldRefConfig)\n646\t\n647\t// WithQuoteStyle creates a FieldRefOption that sets the quote style for field names.\n648\t// The quote style is applied to each field name component (not to array indices or prefixes).\n649\t//\n650\t// Example usage:\n651\t//\n652\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"user.email\", \"response\", WithQuoteStyle(DoubleQuote))\n653\t//\t// Returns: response.\"user\".\"email\"\n654\t//\n655\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"\", WithQuoteStyle(SingleQuote))\n656\t//\t// Returns: 'users'[0].'email'\n657\tfunc WithQuoteStyle(style QuoteStyle) FieldRefOption {\n658\t\treturn func(c *FieldRefConfig) {\n659\t\t\tc.quoteStyle = style\n660\t\t}\n661\t}\n662\t\n663\t// WithPrefix creates a FieldRefOption that sets the prefix for field references.\n664\t// The prefix is added before the field path (e.g., \"response\", \"request\", \"data\").\n665\t// The prefix is not affected by quote styles and is added as-is.\n666\t//\n667\t// If both the prefix parameter and WithPrefix option are provided, the option takes precedence.\n668\t//\n669\t// Example usage:\n670\t//\n671\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"user.email\", \"\", WithPrefix(\"response\"))\n672\t//\t// Returns: response.user.email\n673\t//\n674\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"email\", \"\", WithPrefix(\"request\"))\n675\t//\t// Returns: request.email\n676\t//\n677\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"\", WithPrefix(\"data\"), WithQuoteStyle(DoubleQuote))\n678\t//\t// Returns: data.\"users\"[0].\"email\"\n679\tfunc WithPrefix(prefix string) FieldRefOption {\n680\t\treturn func(c *FieldRefConfig) {\n681\t\t\tc.prefix = prefix\n682\t\t}\n683\t}\n684\t\n685\t// =============================================================================\n686\t// FIELD PATH FORMATTING\n687\t// =============================================================================\n688\t\n689\t// FormatFieldPath formats a field path for consistent display in error messages.\n690\t// This function handles nested field paths, array indices, and provides graceful\n691\t// handling of edge cases like empty paths or invalid formats.\n692\t//\n693\t// Parameters:\n694\t//   - fieldPath: The field path to format (e.g., \"user.email\", \"users.0.email\", \"data.items[5]\")\n695\t//\n696\t// Returns a formatted field reference string ready for use in error messages.\n697\t// Empty or invalid paths return \"(unknown field)\" for consistent error display.\n698\t//\n699\t// Example usage:\n700\t//\n701\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"user.email\")\n702\t//\t// Returns: \"user.email\"\n703\t//\n704\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"users.0.email\")\n705\t//\t// Returns: \"users[0].email\"\n706\t//\n707\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"data.items[5]\")\n708\t//\t// Returns: \"data.items[5]\"\n709\t//\n710\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"\")\n711\t//\t// Returns: \"(unknown field)\"\n712\tfunc FormatFieldPath(fieldPath string) string {\n713\t\tfieldPath = strings.TrimSpace(fieldPath)\n714\t\n715\t\t// Handle empty path\n716\t\tif fieldPath == \"\" {\n717\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n718\t\t}\n719\t\n720\t\t// Parse and normalize the field path\n721\t\treturn normalizeFieldPath(fieldPath)\n722\t}\n723\t\n724\t// normalizeFieldPath processes a field path and normalizes array notation.\n725\t// It converts \"users.0.email\" to \"users[0].email\" format for consistency.\n726\tfunc normalizeFieldPath(path string) string {\n727\t\tif path == \"\" {\n728\t\t\treturn \"\"\n729\t\t}\n730\t\n731\t\tparts := strings.Split(path, \".\")\n732\t\tvar result []string\n733\t\n734\t\tfor _, part := range parts {\n735\t\t\tpart = strings.TrimSpace(part)\n736\t\t\tif part == \"\" {\n737\t\t\t\tcontinue\n738\t\t\t}\n739\t\n740\t\t\t// Check if this part is a numeric index (array accessor)\n741\t\t\tif isNumericIndex(part) {\n742\t\t\t\t// Convert \".0\" to \"[0]\" format\n743\t\t\t\tif len(result) > 0 {\n744\t\t\t\t\t// Append to previous part as array index\n745\t\t\t\t\tlastIdx := len(result) - 1\n746\t\t\t\t\tresult[lastIdx] = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s[%s]\", result[lastIdx], part)\n747\t\t\t\t} else {\n748\t\t\t\t\t// Standalone index at start - keep as is\n749\t\t\t\t\tresult = append(result, part)\n750\t\t\t\t}\n751\t\t\t} else if containsArrayNotation(part) {\n752\t\t\t\t// Already has array notation like \"items[5]\" - keep as is\n753\t\t\t\tresult = append(result, part)\n754\t\t\t} else {\n755\t\t\t\t// Regular field name\n756\t\t\t\tresult = append(result, part)\n757\t\t\t}\n758\t\t}\n759\t\n760\t\tif len(result) == 0 {\n761\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n762\t\t}\n763\t\n764\t\treturn strings.Join(result, \".\")\n765\t}\n766\t\n767\t// isNumericIndex checks if a string represents a numeric array index.\n768\tfunc isNumericIndex(s string) bool {\n769\t\tif s == \"\" {\n770\t\t\treturn false\n771\t\t}\n772\t\n773\t\t// Handle negative numbers (though unusual for indices)\n774\t\tif s[0] == '-' {\n775\t\t\tif len(s) == 1 {\n776\t\t\t\treturn false\n777\t\t\t}\n778\t\t\ts = s[1:]\n779\t\t}\n780\t\n781\t\t// Check if all characters are digits\n782\t\tfor _, r := range s {\n783\t\t\tif r < '0' || r > '9' {\n784\t\t\t\treturn false\n785\t\t\t}\n786\t\t}\n787\t\n788\t\treturn true\n789\t}\n790\t\n791\t// containsArrayNotation checks if a string already contains array notation like \"[5]\".\n792\tfunc containsArrayNotation(s string) bool {\n793\t\treturn strings.Contains(s, \"[\")\n794\t}\n795\t\n796\t// FormatFieldReference creates a standardized field reference string with an optional prefix.\n797\t// This function formats field paths for consistent display in error messages, normalizing\n798\t// array notation and handling edge cases gracefully. It supports optional quote styles\n799\t// for field names through variadic options.\n800\t//\n801\t// Parameters:\n802\t//   - fieldPath: The field path to format (e.g., \"user.email\", \"users.0.email\", \"data.items[5]\")\n803\t//   - prefix: Optional field prefix to add (e.g., \"request\", \"response\", \"\" for no prefix)\n804\t//   - options: Optional functions to configure formatting (e.g., WithQuoteStyle(DoubleQuote))\n805\t//\n806\t// Returns a formatted field reference string with array indices in bracket notation.\n807\t// Empty or invalid paths return \"(unknown field)\" (or \"prefix.(unknown field)\" if prefix provided).\n808\t//\n809\t// Example usage:\n810\t//\n811\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"email\", \"\")\n812\t//\t// Returns: \"email\"\n813\t//\n814\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"\")\n815\t//\t// Returns: \"users[0].email\"\n816\t//\n817\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"email\", \"request\")\n818\t//\t// Returns: \"request.email\"\n819\t//\n820\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"response\")\n821\t//\t// Returns: \"response.users[0].email\"\n822\t//\n823\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"user.email\", \"response\", WithQuoteStyle(DoubleQuote))\n824\t//\t// Returns: response.\"user\".\"email\"\n825\t//\n826\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"\", WithQuoteStyle(SingleQuote))\n827\t//\t// Returns: 'users'[0].'email'\n828\t//\n829\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"data.items\", \"\", WithQuoteStyle(Backtick))\n830\t//\t// Returns: `data`.`items`\n831\t//\n832\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"\", \"\")\n833\t//\t// Returns: \"(unknown field)\"\n834\t//\n835\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"\", \"request\")\n836\t//\t// Returns: \"request\"\n837\tfunc FormatFieldReference(fieldPath string, prefix string, options ...FieldRefOption) string {\n838\t\tfieldPath = strings.TrimSpace(fieldPath)\n839\t\tprefix = strings.TrimSpace(prefix)\n840\t\n841\t\t// Apply options\n842\t\tconfig := &FieldRefConfig{quoteStyle: NoQuote, prefix: \"\"}\n843\t\tfor _, opt := range options {\n844\t\t\topt(config)\n845\t\t}\n846\t\n847\t\t// If prefix was set via option, use it (overrides the direct parameter)\n848\t\tif config.prefix != \"\" {\n849\t\t\tprefix = config.prefix\n850\t\t}\n851\t\n852\t\t// Handle empty field path\n853\t\tif fieldPath == \"\" {\n854\t\t\tif prefix != \"\" {\n855\t\t\t\t// Normalize prefix even when field is \"(unknown field)\"\n874\t\t}\n875\t\n876\t\t// Apply quote style if specified\n877\t\tif config.quoteStyle != NoQuote {\n878\t\t\tnormalizedPath = applyQuoteStyle(normalizedPath, FormatFieldPath(prefix)\n885\t\n886\t\t\t// If prefix normalizes to unknown field, just return the field path\n887\t\t\tif normalizedPrefix == \"(unknown field)\" {\n888\t\t\t\treturn normalizedPath\n889\t\t\t}\n890\t\n891\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s.%s\", normalizedPrefix, normalizedPath)\n892\t\t}\n893\t\n894\t\treturn normalizedPath\n895\t}\n896\t\n897\t// applyQuoteStyle QuoteStyle) string {\n900\t\tif path == \"(unknown field)\" || path == \"\" {\n901\t\t\treturn path\n902\t\t}\n903\t\n904\t\tvar result strings.Builder\n905\t\tvar fieldName r == '[':\n923\t\t\t\t// Start of array index, flush field name first\n924\t\t\t\tif fieldName.Len() > 0 FormatValidationErrorWithDetails functionality with a more ergonomic interface.\ntype ValidationFormatter struct {\n\tvalidationType string\n\texpected       interface{}\n\tactual         interface{}\n\tcontext        string\n\tresponseSnippet string\n\tfieldName      string\n\tpatternDetails string\n\trangeInfo      string\n\tvalidationDetails []string\n\tcustomSuggestions \"error_message\", \"content_type\")\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tformatter := validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code\").\n//\t    WithExpected(200).\n//\t    WithActual(404).\n//\t    WithContext(\"GET /api/users\").\n//\t    Format()\nfunc NewValidationFormatter(validationType string) *ValidationFormatter {\n\treturn &ValidationFormatter{\n\t\tvalidationType: validationType,\n\t}\n}\n\n// WithExpected sets the expected value for validation.\nfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithExpected(expected interface{}) *ValidationFormatter {\n\tvf.expected = expected\n\treturn vf\n}\n\n// WithActual sets the actual value received during validation.\nfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithActual(actual interface{}) *ValidationFormatter {\n\tvf.actual = actual\n\treturn vf\n}\n\n// WithContext sets additional context about the validation operation.\n// Context can include information like the endpoint being tested, operation type, etc.\nfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithContext(context string) *ValidationFormatter {\n\tvf.context = context\n\treturn vf\n}\n\n// WithResponseSnippet sets a truncated excerpt from the response for debugging.\nfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithResponseSnippet(snippet string) *ValidationFormatter {\n\tvf.responseSnippet = snippet\n\treturn vf\n}\n\n// WithFieldName sets the specific field name where the error was found.\n// This is particularly useful for error message validation.\nfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithFieldName(fieldName string) *ValidationFormatter {\n\tvf.fieldName = fieldName\n\treturn vf\n}\n\n// WithPatternDetails sets information about pattern matching failures.\n// Use this when validating error messages against regex patterns.\nfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithPatternDetails(details string) *ValidationFormatter {\n\tvf.patternDetails = details\n\treturn vf\n}\n\n// WithRangeInfo sets range boundaries for range validation failures.\n// Use this when validating against status code ranges (e.g., \"4xx\", \"5xx\").\nfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithRangeInfo(info string) *ValidationFormatter {\n\tvf.rangeInfo = info\n\treturn vf\n}\n\n// WithValidationDetails(details ...string) *ValidationFormatter {\n\tvf.validationDetails = append(vf.validationDetails, details...)\n\treturn vf\n}\n\n// WithSuggestions sets custom suggestions for fixing the validation failure.\n// If not set, suggestions will be auto-generated based on validation type and values.\n// Use this to override auto-generated suggestions with domain-specific guidance.\nfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithSuggestions(suggestions ...string) *ValidationFormatter {\n\tvf.customSuggestions = append(vf.customSuggestions, suggestions...)\n\treturn vf\n}\n\n// Format creates the final ValidationError with all configured options.\n// Returns a ValidationError that implements the error interface.\nfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) Format() ValidationError {\n\tsuggestions := vf.customSuggestions\n\tif len(suggestions) == 0 {\n\t\t// Use auto-generated suggestions if no custom ones provided\n\t\tsuggestions = generateSuggestions(vf.validationType, vf.expected, vf.actual)\n\t}\n\n\t// Construct ValidationError directly to support custom suggestions\n\treturn ValidationError{\n\t\tErrorType:         vf.validationType,\n\t\tExpected:          vf.expected,\n\t\tActual:            vf.actual,\n\t\tContext:           vf.context,\n\t\tResponseSnippet:   vf.responseSnippet,\n\t\tFieldName:         vf.fieldName,\n\t\tPatternDetails:    vf.patternDetails,\n\t\tRangeInfo:         vf.rangeInfo,\n\t\tValidationDetails: vf.validationDetails,\n\t\tSuggestions:       suggestions,\n\t}\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatStatusCodeError creates a validation error for HTTP status code mismatches.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - FormatCustomValidationError creates a validation error with full customization.\n//\n// This function allows complete control over all validation error fields, including\n// custom suggestions. FormatCustomValidationError(\n\tvalidationType string,\n\texpected, actual interface{},\n\toptions ...FormatOption,\n) ValidationError {\n\tconfig := &FormatConfig{}\n\tfor _, opt := range options formatter.WithContext(config.Context)\n\t}\n\tif config.ResponseSnippet != \"\" {\n\t\tformatter = formatter.WithResponseSnippet(config.ResponseSnippet)\n\t}\n\tif config.FieldName != \"\" {\n\t\tformatter = formatter.WithFieldName(config.FieldName)\n\t}\n\tif config.PatternDetails != \"\" {\n\t\tformatter = formatter.WithPatternDetails(config.PatternDetails)\n\t}\n\tif config.RangeInfo != \"\" {\n\t\tformatter = formatter.WithRangeInfo(config.RangeInfo)\n\t}\n\tif len(config.ValidationDetails) > 0 {\n\t\tformatter = formatter.WithValidationDetails(config.ValidationDetails...)\n\t}\n\tif len(config.Suggestions) > 0 {\n\t\tformatter = formatter.WithSuggestions(config.Suggestions...)\n\t}\n\n\treturn formatter.Format()\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// FORMAT OPTIONS FOR CUSTOM VALIDATION\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatConfig holds configuration options for validation error formatting.\ntype FormatConfig struct {\n\tContext            string\n\tResponseSnippet    string\n\tFieldName          string\n\tPatternDetails     string\n\tRangeInfo          string\n\tValidationDetails  []string\n\tSuggestions        []string\n}\n\n// WithResponseSnippet(snippet string) FormatOption {\n\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n\t\tc.ResponseSnippet = snippet\n\t}\n}\n\n// WithFieldName creates a FormatOption that sets the field name.\nfunc WithFieldName(fieldName string) FormatOption {\n\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n\t\tc.FieldName = fieldName\n\t}\n}\n\n// WithPatternDetails creates a FormatOption that sets pattern details.\nfunc WithPatternDetails(details string) FormatOption {\n\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n\t\tc.PatternDetails = details\n\t}\n}\n\n// WithRangeInfo creates a FormatOption that sets range information.\nfunc WithRangeInfo(info string) FormatOption {\n\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n\t\tc.RangeInfo = info\n\t}\n}\n\n// WithValidationDetails creates a FormatOption that adds validation details.\nfunc WithValidationDetails(details ...string) FormatOption {\n\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n\t\tc.ValidationDetails = append(c.ValidationDetails, details...)\n\t}\n}\n\n// WithSuggestions creates a FormatOption that sets custom suggestions.\nfunc WithSuggestions(suggestions ...string) FormatOption {\n\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n\t\tc.Suggestions = =============================================================================\n\n// getRangeInfo extracts range information from a pattern string.\nfunc getRangeInfo(pattern string) (min, max int, desc string, err error) {\n\tmin, max, err = ParseStatusCodeRange(pattern)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn 0, 0, \"\", err\n\t}\n\n\tdesc, err = GetStatusCodeRangeDescription(pattern)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\t// Return parsed range even if description fails\n\t\tdesc = \"\"\n\t}\n\n\treturn min, max, desc, nil\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR TYPE VALIDATION TRACKING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// errorTypeTracker tracks invalid error types encountered during validation.\n// This helps identify typos, deprecated error types, or configuration issues.\ntype errorTypeTracker struct {\n\tmu       sync.RWMutex\n\tinvalidTypes map[string]int // error type -> count\n}\n\n// global tracker instance\nvar globalErrorTypeTracker = &errorTypeTracker{\n\tinvalidTypes: make(map[string]int),\n}\n\n// TrackInvalidErrorType records an invalid error type for later analysis.\n// This is called automatically by FormatError when an error type doesn't\n// match any known ErrorType enum value.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errorType: The invalid error type string that was encountered\n//\n// Returns the current count for this error type (how many times it's been seen).\nfunc TrackInvalidErrorType(errorType string) int {\n\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Unlock()\n\n\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes[errorType]++\n\treturn globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes[errorType]\n}\n\n// GetInvalidErrorTypes returns a snapshot of all invalid error types encountered\n// and their occurrence counts. This is useful for debugging and identifying\n// configuration issues.\n//\n// Returns a copy of the invalid types map to avoid concurrent modification issues.\nfunc GetInvalidErrorTypes() map[string]int {\n\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RLock()\n\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RUnlock()\n\n\tresult := make(map[string]int, len(globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes))\n\tfor k, v := range globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes {\n\t\tresult[k] = v\n\t}\n\treturn result\n}\n\n// ResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking clears all tracked invalid error types.\n// This is primarily useful for testing to ensure a clean state between tests.\nfunc ResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking() {\n\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Unlock()\n\n\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes = make(map[string]int)\n}\n\n// InvalidErrorTypeCount returns the total number of invalid error types tracked\n// (not the total occurrences, but the number of unique invalid types).\nfunc InvalidErrorTypeCount() int {\n\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RLock()\n\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RUnlock()\n\n\treturn len(globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes)\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// BASIC ERROR MESSAGE FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatError creates a basic formatted error message string.\n// This is a simplified formatting function for basic error message display.\n// Unlike the ValidationFormatter which returns ValidationError structs,\n// this function returns a simple formatted string for quick error display.\n//\n// This function supports both string-based error types (for backward compatibility)\n// and validates them against the ErrorType enum for type-safe error classification.\n// The ErrorType enum provides consistent error classification across all validation contexts.\n//\n// Error Type Validation:\n//   - String error types are validated against the ErrorType enum (e.g., \"required\", \"format\", \"range\")\n//   - If the error type is not a recognized ErrorType, it is tracked for debugging purposes\n//   - Invalid error types do NOT cause errors - the original string is still used for backward compatibility\n//   - To check what invalid error types have been encountered, use GetInvalidErrorTypes()\n//   - To reset tracking between tests, use ResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking()\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errorType: The type/category of error (e.g., \"required\", \"format\", \"status_code\")\n//   - message: The error message content\n//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred (can be empty string)\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message string with consistent structure.\n// Handles empty/nil inputs gracefully by returning a default format.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\t// Valid error types (recognized by ErrorType enum)\n//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\"required\", \"This field is required\", \"email\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: This field is required\"\n//\n//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\"format\", \"Invalid email format\", \"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[format] Invalid email format\"\n//\n//\t// Custom/unknown error types (still work, but are tracked)\n//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\"custom_validation\", \"Custom check failed\", \"field\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[custom_validation] field: Custom check failed\"\n//\t// \"custom_validation\" is now tracked as an invalid error type\n//\n//\t// Check what invalid types have been encountered\n//\tinvalidTypes := validate.GetInvalidErrorTypes()\n//\t// Returns: map[string]int{\"custom_validation\": 1, ...}\n//\n//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\"\", \"Something went wrong\", \"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[error] Something went wrong\"\n//\n//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\"validation\", \"\", \"email\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[validation] email: email validation failed\"\nfunc FormatError(errorType string, message string, fieldName ...string) string {\n\t// Validate string errorType against ErrorType enum\n\t// This ensures that string-based error types are checked for validity\n\t// while maintaining backward compatibility with any string value\n\tvalidatedType := ErrorTypeFromString(errorType)\n\n\t// Track invalid error types for debugging\n\t// If ErrorTypeFromString returns ErrTypeUnknown but the original wasn't \"unknown\",\n\t// it means we encountered an unrecognized error type string\n\tif validatedType == ErrTypeUnknown && errorType != \"\" && errorType != \"unknown\" {\n\t\tTrackInvalidErrorType(errorType)\n\t}\n\n\t// Extract field name from variadic args\n\tfieldNameStr := \"\"\n\tif len(fieldName) > 0 {\n\t\tfieldNameStr = fieldName[0]\n\t}\n\n\t// Handle empty message - use fallback\n\tif message == \"\" {\n\t\t// Check if field name was provided\n\t\tif fieldNameStr != \"\" {\n\t\t\tmessage = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed\", fieldNameStr)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tmessage = \"(no message provided)\"\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Handle empty errorType - use fallback\n\tif errorType == \"\" {\n\t\terrorType = \"error\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Use FormatErrorMessage with the original error type string\n\t// This maintains backward compatibility - we always use the original string\n\t// even if it wasn't a recognized ErrorType enum value\n\treturn FormatErrorMessage(errorType, message, fieldNameStr)\n}\n\n// FormatErrorWithType creates a basic formatted error message string using ErrorType enum.\n// This function provides type-safe error classification using the ErrorType enum\n// instead of string-based error types.\n//\n// This is the preferred method for error formatting when you have an ErrorType enum\n// value, as it ensures consistent error classification and prevents typos.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value (e.g., ErrTypeRequired, ErrTypeFormat)\n//   - message: The error message content\n//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred (can be empty string)\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message string with consistent structure.\n// Handles empty/nil inputs gracefully by returning a default format.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorWithType(validate.ErrTypeRequired, \"Field is required\", \"email\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: Field is required\"\n//\n//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorWithType(validate.ErrTypeFormat, \"Invalid email format\", \"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[format] Invalid email format\"\n//\n//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorWithType(validate.ErrTypeRange, \"Value out of range\", \"age\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[range] age: Value out of range\"\nfunc FormatErrorWithType(errorType ErrorType, message string, fieldName string) string {\n\t// Convert ErrorType enum to string for formatting\n\terrorTypeStr := errorType.String()\n\n\t// Handle empty message - use fallback\n\tif message == \"\" {\n\t\t// Check if field name was provided\n\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n\t\t\tmessage = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed\", fieldName)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tmessage = \"(no message provided)\"\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Use FormatErrorMessage for consistent formatting\n\treturn FormatErrorMessage(errorTypeStr, message, fieldName)\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// FIELD REFERENCE QUOTE STYLES\n// =============================================================================\n\n// QuoteStyle defines the quoting style for field names in field references.\ntype QuoteStyle string\n\nconst (\n\t// NoQuote applies no quotes to field names\n\tNoQuote QuoteStyle = \"\"\n\t// SingleQuote applies single quotes around field names\n\tSingleQuote QuoteStyle = \"'\"\n\t// DoubleQuote applies double quotes around field names\n\tDoubleQuote QuoteStyle = \"\\\"\"\n\t// Backtick applies backtick quotes around field names\n\tBacktick QuoteStyle = \"`\"\n)\n\n// FieldRefConfig holds configuration options for field reference formatting.\ntype FieldRefConfig struct {\n\tquoteStyle QuoteStyle\n\tprefix     string\n}\n\n// FieldRefOption is a function that configures a FieldRefConfig.\ntype FieldRefOption func(*FieldRefConfig)\n\n// WithQuoteStyle creates a FieldRefOption that sets the quote style for field names.\n// The quote style is applied to each field name component (not to array indices or prefixes).\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"user.email\", \"response\", WithQuoteStyle(DoubleQuote))\n//\t// Returns: response.\"user\".\"email\"\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"\", WithQuoteStyle(SingleQuote))\n//\t// Returns: 'users'[0].'email'\nfunc WithQuoteStyle(style QuoteStyle) FieldRefOption {\n\treturn func(c *FieldRefConfig) {\n\t\tc.quoteStyle = style\n\t}\n}\n\n// WithPrefix creates a FieldRefOption that sets the prefix for field references.\n// The prefix is added before the field path (e.g., \"response\", \"request\", \"data\").\n// The prefix is not affected by quote styles and is added as-is.\n//\n// If both the prefix parameter and WithPrefix option are provided, the option takes precedence.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"user.email\", \"\", WithPrefix(\"response\"))\n//\t// Returns: response.user.email\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"email\", \"\", WithPrefix(\"request\"))\n//\t// Returns: request.email\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"\", WithPrefix(\"data\"), WithQuoteStyle(DoubleQuote))\n//\t// Returns: data.\"users\"[0].\"email\"\nfunc WithPrefix(prefix string) FieldRefOption {\n\treturn func(c *FieldRefConfig) {\n\t\tc.prefix = prefix\n\t}\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// FIELD PATH FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatFieldPath formats a field path for consistent display in error messages.\n// This function handles nested field paths, array indices, and provides graceful\n// handling of edge cases like empty paths or invalid formats.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fieldPath: The field path to format (e.g., \"user.email\", \"users.0.email\", \"data.items[5]\")\n//\n// Returns a formatted field reference string ready for use in error messages.\n// Empty or invalid paths return \"(unknown field)\" for consistent error display.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"user.email\")\n//\t// Returns: \"user.email\"\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"users.0.email\")\n//\t// Returns: \"users[0].email\"\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"data.items[5]\")\n//\t// Returns: \"data.items[5]\"\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"(unknown field)\"\nfunc FormatFieldPath(fieldPath string) string {\n\tfieldPath = strings.TrimSpace(fieldPath)\n\n\t// Handle empty path\n\tif fieldPath == \"\" {\n\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Parse and normalize the field path\n\treturn normalizeFieldPath(fieldPath)\n}\n\n// normalizeFieldPath processes a field path and normalizes array notation.\n// It converts \"users.0.email\" to \"users[0].email\" format for consistency.\nfunc normalizeFieldPath(path string) string {\n\tif path == \"\" {\n\t\treturn \"\"\n\t}\n\n\tparts := strings.Split(path, \".\")\n\tvar result []string\n\n\tfor _, part := range parts {\n\t\tpart = strings.TrimSpace(part)\n\t\tif part == \"\" {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Check if this part is a numeric index (array accessor)\n\t\tif isNumericIndex(part) {\n\t\t\t// Convert \".0\" to \"[0]\" format\n\t\t\tif len(result) > 0 {\n\t\t\t\t// Append to previous part as array index\n\t\t\t\tlastIdx := len(result) - 1\n\t\t\t\tresult[lastIdx] = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s[%s]\", result[lastIdx], part)\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\t// Standalone index at start - keep as is\n\t\t\t\tresult = append(result, part)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t} else if containsArrayNotation(part) {\n\t\t\t// Already has array notation like \"items[5]\" - keep as is\n\t\t\tresult = append(result, part)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t// Regular field name\n\t\t\tresult = append(result, part)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif len(result) == 0 {\n\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n\t}\n\n\treturn strings.Join(result, \".\")\n}\n\n// isNumericIndex checks if a string represents a numeric array index.\nfunc isNumericIndex(s string) bool {\n\tif s == \"\" {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\t// Handle negative numbers (though unusual for indices)\n\tif s[0] == '-' {\n\t\tif len(s) == 1 {\n\t\t\treturn false\n\t\t}\n\t\ts = s[1:]\n\t}\n\n\t// Check if all characters are digits\n\tfor _, r := range s {\n\t\tif r < '0' || r > '9' {\n\t\t\treturn false\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn true\n}\n\n// containsArrayNotation checks if a string already contains array notation like \"[5]\".\nfunc containsArrayNotation(s string) bool {\n\treturn strings.Contains(s, \"[\")\n}\n\n// FormatFieldReference creates a standardized field reference string with an optional prefix.\n// This function formats field paths for consistent display in error messages, normalizing\n// array notation and handling edge cases gracefully. It supports optional quote styles\n// for field names through variadic options.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fieldPath: The field path to format (e.g., \"user.email\", \"users.0.email\", \"data.items[5]\")\n//   - prefix: Optional field prefix to add (e.g., \"request\", \"response\", \"\" for no prefix)\n//   - options: Optional functions to configure formatting (e.g., WithQuoteStyle(DoubleQuote))\n//\n// Returns a formatted field reference string with array indices in bracket notation.\n// Empty or invalid paths return \"(unknown field)\" (or \"prefix.(unknown field)\" if prefix provided).\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"email\", \"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"email\"\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"users[0].email\"\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"email\", \"request\")\n//\t// Returns: \"request.email\"\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"response\")\n//\t// Returns: \"response.users[0].email\"\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"user.email\", \"response\", WithQuoteStyle(DoubleQuote))\n//\t// Returns: response.\"user\".\"email\"\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"\", WithQuoteStyle(SingleQuote))\n//\t// Returns: 'users'[0].'email'\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"data.items\", \"\", WithQuoteStyle(Backtick))\n//\t// Returns: `data`.`items`\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"\", \"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"(unknown field)\"\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"\", \"request\")\n//\t// Returns: \"request\"\nfunc FormatFieldReference(fieldPath string, prefix string, options ...FieldRefOption) string {\n\tfieldPath = strings.TrimSpace(fieldPath)\n\tprefix = strings.TrimSpace(prefix)\n\n\t// Apply options\n\tconfig := &FieldRefConfig{quoteStyle: NoQuote, prefix: \"\"}\n\tfor _, opt := range options {\n\t\topt(config)\n\t}\n\n\t// If prefix was set via option, use it (overrides the direct parameter)\n\tif config.prefix != \"\" {\n\t\tprefix = config.prefix\n\t}\n\n\t// Handle empty field path\n\tif fieldPath == \"\" {\n\t\tif prefix != \"\" {\n\t\t\t// Normalize prefix even when field is empty\n\t\t\tnormalizedPrefix := FormatFieldPath(prefix)\n\t\t\tif normalizedPrefix != \"(unknown field)\" {\n\t\t\t\treturn normalizedPrefix\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn prefix\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Normalize field path to handle array indices\n\tnormalizedPath := FormatFieldPath(fieldPath)\n\n\t// Check if normalization resulted in unknown field indicator\n\tif normalizedPath == \"(unknown field)\" {\n\t\tif prefix != \"\" {\n\t\t\treturn prefix\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Apply quote style if specified\n\tif config.quoteStyle != NoQuote {\n\t\tnormalizedPath = applyQuoteStyle(normalizedPath, config.quoteStyle)\n\t}\n\n\t// Add prefix if provided\n\tif prefix != \"\" {\n\t\t// Normalize prefix as well in case it contains array indices\n\t\tnormalizedPrefix := FormatFieldPath(prefix)\n\n\t\t// If prefix normalizes to unknown field, just return the field path\n\t\tif normalizedPrefix == \"(unknown field)\" {\n\t\t\treturn normalizedPath\n\t\t}\n\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s.%s\", normalizedPrefix, normalizedPath)\n\t}\n\n\treturn normalizedPath\n}\n\n// applyQuoteStyle applies quotes to field names in a normalized path.\n// Array indices are NOT quoted (e.g., \"users[0].email\" becomes \"'users'[0].'email'\").\nfunc applyQuoteStyle(path string, style QuoteStyle) string {\n\tif path == \"(unknown field)\" || path == \"\" {\n\t\treturn path\n\t}\n\n\tvar result strings.Builder\n\tvar fieldName strings.Builder\n\tinBrackets := false\n\n\tfor i := 0; i < len(path); i++ {\n\t\tr := rune(path[i])\n\n\t\tswitch {\n\t\tcase r == '.':\n\t\t\t// End of field name, add quoted version\n\t\t\tif fieldName.Len() > 0 {\n\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(fieldName.String())\n\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n\t\t\t\tfieldName.Reset()\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tresult.WriteRune('.')\n\n\t\tcase r == '[':\n\t\t\t// Start of array index, flush field name first\n\t\t\tif fieldName.Len() > 0 {\n\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(fieldName.String())\n\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n\t\t\t\tfieldName.Reset()\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tresult.WriteRune('[')\n\t\t\tinBrackets = true\n\n\t\tcase r == ']':\n\t\t\t// End of array index, don't quote\n\t\t\tresult.WriteRune(']')\n\t\t\tinBrackets = false\n\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\t// Only accumulate field name characters when NOT inside brackets\n\t\t\tif !inBrackets {\n\t\t\t\tfieldName.WriteRune(r)\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tresult.WriteRune(r)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Don't forget the last field name\n\tif fieldName.Len() > 0 {\n\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n\t\tresult.WriteString(fieldName.String())\n\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n\t}\n\n\treturn result.String()\n}\n\n","numLines":958,"startLine":1,"totalLines":958}}}

functionality with a more ergonomic interface.\ntype ValidationFormatter struct {\n\tvalidationType string\n\texpected       interface{}\n\tactual         interface{}\n\tcontext        string\n\tresponseSnippet string\n\tfieldName      string\n\tpatternDetails string\n\trangeInfo      string\n\tvalidationDetails []string\n\tcustomSuggestions []string\n}\n\n// NewValidationFormatter \"content_type\")\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tformatter := validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code\").\n//\t    WithExpected(200).\n//\t    WithActual(404).\n//\t    WithContext(\"GET /api/users\").\n//\t    Format()\nfunc NewValidationFormatter(validationType string) *ValidationFormatter {\n\treturn &ValidationFormatter{\n\t\tvalidationType: validationType,\n\t}\n}\n\n// WithValidationDetails(details ...string) *ValidationFormatter {\n\tvf.validationDetails = append(vf.validationDetails, details...)\n\treturn vf\n}\n\n// WithSuggestions sets custom suggestions for fixing the validation failure.\n// If not set, suggestions will be auto-generated based on validation type and values.\n// Use this to override auto-generated suggestions with domain-specific guidance.\nfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithSuggestions(suggestions ...string) *ValidationFormatter {\n\tvf.customSuggestions = append(vf.customSuggestions, suggestions...)\n\treturn vf\n}\n\n// Format creates the final ValidationError with all configured options.\n// Returns a ValidationError that implements the error interface.\nfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) Format() ValidationError {\n\tsuggestions := vf.customSuggestions\n\tif len(suggestions) == 0 {\n\t\t// Use auto-generated suggestions if no custom ones provided\n\t\tsuggestions = generateSuggestions(vf.validationType, vf.expected, vf.actual)\n\t}\n\n\t// Construct ValidationError directly to support custom suggestions\n\treturn ValidationError{\n\t\tErrorType:         vf.validationType,\n\t\tExpected:          vf.expected,\n\t\tActual:            vf.actual,\n\t\tContext:           vf.context,\n\t\tResponseSnippet:   vf.responseSnippet,\n\t\tFieldName:         vf.fieldName,\n\t\tPatternDetails:    vf.patternDetails,\n\t\tRangeInfo:         vf.rangeInfo,\n\t\tValidationDetails: vf.validationDetails,\n\t\tSuggestions:       suggestions,\n\t}\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// CONVENIENCE FUNCTIONS FOR COMMON VALIDATION SCENARIOS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatStatusCodeError creates a validation error for HTTP status code mismatches.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - expected: The expected status code (e.g., 200) or codes (e.g., []int{200, 201})\n//   - actual: The actual status code received\n//   - context: Optional context about the request (e.g., \"GET /api/users\")\n//\n// Returns a ValidationError with appropriate suggestions.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terr := validate.FormatStatusCodeError(200, 404, \"GET /api/users/123\")\n//\t// Output:\n//\t// status_code validation failed\n//\t//   Expected: 200 (OK)\n//\t//   Actual:   404 (Not Found)\n//\t//   Context:  GET /api/users/123\n//\t//   Suggestions:\n//\t//     - Verify the endpoint URL is correct\n//\t//     - Check if the resource ID or identifier exists\n//\t//     - Ensure the resource hasn't been deleted or moved\nfunc FormatStatusCodeError(expected interface{}, actual int, context string) ValidationError {\n\treturn NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code\").\n\t\tWithExpected(expected).\n\t\tWithActual(actual).\n\t\tWithContext(context).\n\t\tFormat()\n}\n\n// FormatErrorMessageError creates a validation error for error message pattern mismatches.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - expectedPattern: The pattern that was expected (e.g., \"invalid.*token\", \"not found\")\n//   - actualMessage: The actual error message received\n//   - fieldName: The field where the error was found (e.g., \"error\", \"message\")\n//   - context: Optional context about the validation\n//\n// Returns a ValidationError with appropriate suggestions.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terr := validate.FormatErrorMessageError(\"invalid.*token\", \"access_denied\", \"error\", \"OAuth validation\")\n//\t// Output:\n//\t// error_message validation failed\n//\t//   Expected: invalid.*token\n//\t//   Actual:   access_denied\n//\t//   Context:  OAuth validation\n//\t//   Field:    error\n//\t//   Suggestions:\n//\t//     - Review the error message for specific details\n//\t//     - Check API documentation for this error type\n//\t//     - Verify request parameters match FormatStatusCodeRangeError creates a validation error for status code range mismatches.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - pattern: The range pattern (e.g., \"4xx\", \"5xx\", \"2xx\")\n//   - actual: The actual status code received\n//   - context: Optional context about the validation\n//\n// Returns a ValidationError with appropriate range information and suggestions.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terr := validate.FormatStatusCodeRangeError(\"4xx\", 200, \"error response check\")\n//\t// Output:\n//\t// status_code_range validation failed\n//\t//   Expected: 4xx (400-499)\n//\t//   Actual:   200\n//\t//   Context:  error response check\n//\t//   Suggestions:\n//\t//     - Review request parameters for errors\n//\t//     - Check authentication credentials\n//\t//     - Verify the resource exists and is accessible\nfunc FormatStatusCodeRangeError(pattern string, actual int, context string) ValidationError {\n\tmin, max, desc, err := getRangeInfo(pattern)\n\n\t// Build range info string\n\trangeInfo := fmt.Sprintf(\"%d-%d\", min, max)\n\tif desc != \"\" {\n\t\trangeInfo = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d-%d (%s)\", min, max, desc)\n\t}\n\n\tdetails := []string{}\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tdetails = append(details, fmt.Sprintf(\"Pattern error: %v\", err))\n\t} else {\n\t\tdetails = append(details, fmt.Sprintf(\"Status code %d is outside range %d-%d\", actual, min, max))\n\t}\n\n\tformatter := NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code_range\").\n\t\tWithExpected(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s (%s)\", pattern, desc)).\n\t\tWithActual(actual).\n\t\tWithContext(context).\n\t\tWithValidationDetails(details...)\n\n\t// Only add range info if the pattern is valid\n\tif err == nil {\n\t\tformatter = formatter.WithRangeInfo(rangeInfo)\n\t}\n\n\treturn formatter.Format()\n}\n\n// FormatContentTypeError creates a validation error for Content-Type header mismatches.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - expected: The expected content type (e.g., \"application/json\")\n//   - actual: The actual content type received\n//   - context: Optional context about the validation\n//\n// Returns a ValidationError with appropriate suggestions.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terr := validate.FormatContentTypeError(\"application/json\", \"text/html\", \"API response\")\n//\t// Output:\n//\t//
